# Transcrição: 4. GA- Analysing Ad Flow

## [00:00 - 03:00]

So in this example here, this client have been using DCO testimonials, so dynamic content optimized ads, which are holding a number of different variants of an ad in their mid and bottom of funnel, so warm and hot as you can see here, spent about 10k, decent ROAS overall, but what we wanted to see is what contribution are the DCO ads having overall, where do they sit in the click path journey. Now the reason why this is important is because if these ads are working to convert people at the end of their journey, so for example they have been through the top of the funnel, then come back and converted, that's great. We also want to know if they've contributed elsewhere, maybe someone signed up for an email or something after that as well. So here all I've done is to show the active ads, added a filter ad name contains testimonial, which has just brought up these two campaigns, and I can see the ads here as well. So this particular client have named their ads exactly the same, I do not recommend this because it makes it difficult to know what's going on. There are similar ads but they're also applied to different parts of the funnel as well, so different audiences and in those audiences you have these testimonial ads that have similar elements, but this is also a learning for you guys as well, it's not an account that we're running, but when it comes to naming convention at the top level, the initial setup that we did for them, they're following, so labelling it based on the funnel, which this is our mid and this is our bottom, which objective is being used, CBO versus ABO, that's all fine, and then having an idea of the audiences inside because all of this data gets pushed to Google Analytics, and if you want to read it and understand it, you need to know what's going on, but then every single ad here is exactly the same, but there are different ads running bottom and mid funnel. Anyway, back to looking at this data in Google Analytics, so in order to analyze this, we go into conversions, multi-channel funnels, and top conversion paths, and here I want to know for the campaigns that we're running that contain that in the UTM parameters, what does that look like in terms of click path? So what we do here is slightly different, so the campaign is here, the ad sets here, I'm looking at the ad name, and if you recall from our setup, if I want the ad name in Google Analytics, I have to find the UTM term, that is actually under keyword, so if I do keyword, what Google is showing me here is the top conversion paths and then the keywords, and by the way, when

## [03:00 - 06:04]

you first look at this report, make sure you are looking at what you think you are looking at, because here I just want to look at e-commerce conversions, but by default, Google is showing all conversions, so these are goals that have been set up, I don't want goals, I just want conversions, I'll apply that and you'll see these numbers change as well accordingly, and for this particular journey, I do want two paths or more, that means if a DCO ad was the first and the last click, for example, you know, it's outside of the 30-day tracking window or something like that, I don't want that, I want to see where it's got a multiple path, and you can set all the different paths here too, is good for me. Now that we've got source and medium and we've got keyword, actually I'm going to change this to, in fact, you know, we'll leave it there, that's fine, so now what I can do is I can fill to the keyword in the same way I did it here, so if I just copy that, I can go into my advanced search, so you can choose the dimensions here, so the first one I've selected is source and medium path, which is here, keyword is also here, so I'm going to select keyword containing testimonials DCO and plus apply, now what this will do is I'm asking Google Analytics to show me the source and medium path where that particular keyword was tracked or UTM term, or basically all I'm saying is show me the path where that particular ad was having an impact on the customer journey, so for example in this particular time period 44 of those conversions were generated from, and this is organic, this is referred to as organic Facebook, interpaid Facebook, so that organic post or whatever was happening on organic, the most popular path was the organic ad, not ad, the organic post was sent and then our retargeting on the site has actually generated the conversion or the transaction, so that's interesting as well because from that we can understand part of the journey that it's not just top of our paid funnel that this particular ad is performing, it's actually part of organic as well, here you can see actually the first click was testimonial DCO, so that's the middle bottom of funnel and then it converted through an untracked channel here, so what I would try and understand from this is to get a picture of where the particular ad is in the customer journey and get an idea of what the contribution is across the user journey apart from ads, because bear in mind when you're looking at this data here, so spent 9k generated 25k which is good, this is just showing the ads journey, so this says

## [06:04 - 08:29]

someone's clicked this ad and then converted, it doesn't show what's happening with organic as well, so this will help us to understand whether there's anything else happening outside of the data we breakdown here as well, so I can go into advanced keyword containing testimonials DCO and here what I can do is actually find out the assisted conversion from and it says paid search because our tagging falls under paid search, we'll just do source medium and then I lose my secondary dimension, that's annoying, let's just key that in, you're seeing this live so you actually get an idea of certain things that might not go quite as you expect, this says an FYI there, so where this ad existed, the source medium Facebook, what I'm looking at here is and I have to check this again yet that's fine, so what I'm looking at here is how many conversions were attributed directly as a last click which is 347, 11k as far as Google Analytics is concerned, but then how many conversions occurred where this ad was not the last click, so there's another 5.8, 9k, let's say 6k, we'll round it off, so we're trying to understand the attribution of this ad across other campaigns and flows as well, so this is just an idea to give you a view of trying to understand the different parts of the user journey, the ad you're running and to inspire you to think different as well because some of the insights I share here are trying to prompt you to think about your particular business case and the questions you have, so thinking about your campaign structure, thinking about the ad sets that you're running and the ads, what's going to be most useful for you to have answered and start with that question first, so for example I would like to know, then go into the data and figure out how you can answer that, so certain things you can answer in ads manager, other things you'll want to go into Google Analytics to find out what the answer is to that particular question.
